h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.statedInsightEvidence: Wait — had it right the first time.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will simply correct the arrow direction and move to the next box.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: so the whole bottom layer works like this, every plant box fans out to everything that eats it— [glances up at remaining boxes] which box is the tail for fox?
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully enacts the new forking rule across the bottom layer and proactively identifies the structural requirement for the next level.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The fork stops “one line”: food at the tail each time, eater at each point. Mark the food end on this next box; then split the points.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor explicitly contrasts the learner's 'one line' overgeneralization with a new public mechanism ('the fork' and 'split the points') to redirect the task.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: The fork stops “one line”: food at the tail each time, eater at each point. Mark the food end on this next box; then split the points.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The device is highly specific, directly counters the learner's linear misconception, and provides a clear, actionable spatial rule for drawing the web.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: No pivot learner turn exists to trigger a recognition-contingent adaptation from the tutor.

learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner successfully enacts the new forking rule across the bottom layer and proactively identifies the structural requirement for the next level.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: No pivot learner turn exists to trigger a recognition-contingent adaptation from the tutor.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: The fork stops “one line”: food at the tail each time, eater at each point. Mark the food end on this next box; then split the points.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor explicitly contrasts the learner's 'one line' overgeneralization with a new public mechanism ('the fork' and 'split the points') to redirect the task.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor explicitly contrasts the learner's 'one line' overgeneralization with a new public mechanism ('the fork' and 'split the points') to redirect the task.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism_quality.evidence: The fork stops “one line”: food at the tail each time, eater at each point. Mark the food end on this next box; then split the points.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism_quality.justification: The device is highly specific, directly counters the learner's linear misconception, and provides a clear, actionable spatial rule for drawing the web.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I've put scale factor 2 for all three — side doubles, perimeter doubles, same ratio, so same label.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to merely correct the numerical value of the area scale factor for the specific diagram without generalizing the underlying mathematical relationship.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Side and perimeter under k. Area under k squared. Rule: if the side factor is k, the area factor is k squared — that's what both cases give.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully executes the new sorting device and formulates the generalized algebraic rule, demonstrating a clear actional breakthrough.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Not a different operation. A different quantity being compared. Side length compares 4 cm with 2 cm. Put that under length ratios.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor explicitly contrasts the learner's assumption of a different operation with the reality of a different quantity, introducing the column-sorting mechanism to restructure the learner's approach.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: Not a different operation. A different quantity being compared. Side length compares 4 cm with 2 cm. Put that under length ratios.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The column-sorting device is highly specific, directly resolves the learner's conceptual block about the nature of the scale factor, and provides a clear, actionable criterion for categorization.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: The boxed version is now defensible: length scale factor 2, perimeter scale factor 2, area scale factor 4. The drawing was not the error. The repeated label was.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor validates the learner's newly established dimensional distinction and explicitly closes the loop on the original misconception regarding the drawing versus the label.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: I've put scale factor 2 for all three — side doubles, perimeter doubles, same ratio, so same label.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: Side and perimeter under k. Area under k squared. Rule: if the side factor is k, the area factor is k squared — that's what both cases give.
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner successfully executes the new sorting device and formulates the generalized algebraic rule, demonstrating a clear actional breakthrough.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.evidence: The boxed version is now defensible: length scale factor 2, perimeter scale factor 2, area scale factor 4. The drawing was not the error. The repeated label was.
tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: The tutor validates the learner's newly established dimensional distinction and explicitly closes the loop on the original misconception regarding the drawing versus the label.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: Not a different operation. A different quantity being compared. Side length compares 4 cm with 2 cm. Put that under length ratios.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor explicitly contrasts the learner's assumption of a different operation with the reality of a different quantity, introducing the column-sorting mechanism to restructure the learner's approach.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: Not a different operation. A different quantity being compared. Side length compares 4 cm with 2 cm. Put that under length ratios.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor explicitly contrasts the learner's assumption of a different operation with the reality of a different quantity, introducing the column-sorting mechanism to restructure the learner's approach.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism_quality.evidence: Not a different operation. A different quantity being compared. Side length compares 4 cm with 2 cm. Put that under length ratios.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism_quality.justification: The column-sorting device is highly specific, directly resolves the learner's conceptual block about the nature of the scale factor, and provides a clear, actionable criterion for categorization.

scored.recoheredEarlier: The shortage doesn't come from nowhere. Twelve percent is what members are paying tonight.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to reject the analytical distinction between pump gouging and supply shortage because they see it as a corporate excuse to drop the boycott.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: if the pumps were dry, then stock orders. Because whoever signed off on what they ordered knew the margin they were sitting on.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully applies the tutor's analytical distinction to select the correct upstream evidence while formulating a synthesized political criterion that maintains the original action.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Members paid in both cases. That is not softened. But the proof moves. Forecourt proof for the first charge. Upstream proof for the second.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor takes stock of the learner's resistance by validating that the pain is real and the shortage is a culpable act, then introduces a new public mechanism by separating the epistemic proof from the political pain.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: Pump charge: fuel in the tanks, ordinary queues, ordinary deliveries, twelve percent added because they could. Supply-chain charge: short tanks before the rise, missed delivery, wholesale price up, allocation cut before members reached the pump.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The device is highly specific, validates the learner's political concern to reduce affective resistance, and provides a clear, actionable epistemic criterion that moves the learning forward.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no later tutor turn after the learner's pivot in L3 to adapt to the revised framing.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: The shortage doesn't come from nowhere. Twelve percent is what members are paying tonight.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: if the pumps were dry, then stock orders. Because whoever signed off on what they ordered knew the margin they were sitting on.
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner successfully applies the tutor's analytical distinction to select the correct upstream evidence while formulating a synthesized political criterion that maintains the original action.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no later tutor turn after the learner's pivot in L3 to adapt to the revised framing.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: Members paid in both cases. That is not softened. But the proof moves. Forecourt proof for the first charge. Upstream proof for the second.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor takes stock of the learner's resistance by validating that the pain is real and the shortage is a culpable act, then introduces a new public mechanism by separating the epistemic proof from the political pain.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: Members paid in both cases. That is not softened. But the proof moves. Forecourt proof for the first charge. Upstream proof for the second.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor takes stock of the learner's resistance by validating that the pain is real and the shortage is a culpable act, then introduces a new public mechanism by separating the epistemic proof from the political pain.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism_quality.evidence: Pump charge: fuel in the tanks, ordinary queues, ordinary deliveries, twelve percent added because they could. Supply-chain charge: short tanks before the rise, missed delivery, wholesale price up, allocation cut before members reached the pump.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism_quality.justification: The device is highly specific, validates the learner's political concern to reduce affective resistance, and provides a clear, actionable epistemic criterion that moves the learning forward.

scored.statedInsightEvidence: So — the form being complete isn't the same as... right.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner expects to locate the missing safety instruction within the existing text to satisfy the tutor's verification task.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: is that condition somewhere here that I'm not finding, or is it actually not written?
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner actively tests the text against a new criterion of explicit safety conditions and enacts a search that evaluates the document's structural deficiency rather than just reading it.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The failed search now counts as bench evidence. If the stop condition sits elsewhere, this line must point to it; if no such line can be found, the power-on tick is not a release condition.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor explicitly contrasts the old route of finding the text with a new mechanism that treats the absence of text as positive evidence to withhold the signature.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: The failed search now counts as bench evidence.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The device is highly specific, transforms the learner's textual dead-end into a valid engineering criterion, and provides a clear, actionable rule for the final signature.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: The failed search now counts as bench evidence. If the stop condition sits elsewhere, this line must point to it; if no such line can be found, the power-on tick is not a release condition.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor directly adopts the learner's realization that the text might simply be missing the condition and builds the final evaluation rule around that exact discovery.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: is that condition somewhere here that I'm not finding, or is it actually not written?
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner actively tests the text against a new criterion of explicit safety conditions and enacts a search that evaluates the document's structural deficiency rather than just reading it.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.evidence: The failed search now counts as bench evidence. If the stop condition sits elsewhere, this line must point to it; if no such line can be found, the power-on tick is not a release condition.
tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: The tutor directly adopts the learner's realization that the text might simply be missing the condition and builds the final evaluation rule around that exact discovery.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: The failed search now counts as bench evidence. If the stop condition sits elsewhere, this line must point to it; if no such line can be found, the power-on tick is not a release condition.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor explicitly contrasts the old route of finding the text with a new mechanism that treats the absence of text as positive evidence to withhold the signature.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: The failed search now counts as bench evidence. If the stop condition sits elsewhere, this line must point to it; if no such line can be found, the power-on tick is not a release condition.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor explicitly contrasts the old route of finding the text with a new mechanism that treats the absence of text as positive evidence to withhold the signature.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism_quality.evidence: The failed search now counts as bench evidence.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism_quality.justification: The device is highly specific, transforms the learner's textual dead-end into a valid engineering criterion, and provides a clear, actionable rule for the final signature.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to identify force arrows based on their initial spatial orientation without needing physical grounding.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence:
[places one finger on the bottom edge of the cart drawing and holds it there, eyes moving to the arrows]

[reaches for the friction tile and sets it on the arrow meeting that edge]
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully enacts the new physical constraint to correctly map the force arrow.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Floor names the object. Put one finger on the cart surface the floor touches. Keep it there. Point to the arrow for that contact acting on the cart; then move the friction tile.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor introduces a new physical constraint mechanism but lacks an explicit stock-taking contrast showing the old route failed.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: Put one finger on the cart surface the floor touches. Keep it there. Point to the arrow for that contact acting on the cart; then move the friction tile.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The physical touch constraint is precise, usable, and directly addresses the learner's spatial confusion by grounding the interaction.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn, so there is no recognition-contingent tutor uptake.

scored.statedInsightEvidence: Oh — so just that one entry.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner seeks to identify and correct the specific errors marked in red on their sheet.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: That one's inside one of the rows, not at the top — so that's... row value?
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully enacts the tutor's spatial criterion to classify a new cell as a row value.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor merely reiterates the spatial placement test introduced in the previous turn rather than inventing a new adaptive mechanism.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: No new adaptive mechanism is introduced; the tutor simply restates the previously established placement criterion.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn to which the tutor could adapt.
